key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 operating cash flow $783.8M less capital spending $69.1M ÷ 86.6M diluted shares per ALLE/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260217_rpt20251231_acc0001579241_26_000007.htm
Americas mechanical strength; electronic mix shift; stable pricing

open_questions: Electronic/software option probability band (~$8/sh base) needs channel validation
Revolver draw and variable-rate debt: refinancing plan at Q2 2026 earnings
Confirm whether Q1 2026 EPS decline is timing or margin pressure
thesis: Allegion plc is a global provider of security products for commercial and residential buildings. **[Fact]** FY2025 net revenue was **$4.07 billion**, up **7.9%** from **$3.77 billion** in FY2024. **[Fact]** The company sells mechanical locks, exit devices, electronic access control, and software under brands including Schlage, Von Duprin, and CISA. **[Fact]** Two reportable segments dominate: **Americas** (**$3.22 billion**, 27.9% operating margin) and **International** (**$849 million**, 9.0% margin). **[Fact]** FY2025 operating cash flow was **$783.8 million** minus capital spending of **$69.1 million**, yielding free cash flow of about **$714.7 million** on **86.6 million** diluted shares (**~$8.25** per share). **[Inference]** Allegion earns by specifying products into new construction and retrofit projects where architect and installer relationships create switching costs.
why_market_wrong: The stock trades near **17 times** FY2025 free cash flow per share while Americas revenue grew mid-single digits and electronic mix is rising. **[Inference]** The market may overweight **non-residential construction slowdown** and **import competition** while underweighting **specification-grade mechanical share** and **electronic access upsell**. **[Fact]** Q1 2026 revenue was **$1.03 billion** (+9.7% year over year) with Americas at **$810 million**, suggesting near-term demand remains solid. **[Fact]** Total debt is **$1.98 billion** against cash of **$356 million**; a **$191 million** revolver draw adds variable-rate sensitivity. **[Inference]** No dated Horizon Kinetics predictive attribute exists; the edge is **durable franchise at a fair price**, not a catalyst with a known payoff date.
